title: Email thread view with collapsed-quoted-replies and inline attachments
design_brief: Building email thread view (think Gmail, Hey, Superhuman). Pain point: long threads with quoted text become walls of repeated content.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Showing full quoted text by default
Same visual weight for all messages in thread
ux_reasoning: Latest message expanded by default. Previous messages collapsed to one-line summary (sender + first 80 chars + timestamp). Click summary expands. Quoted text within a message auto-collapses to '> Show quoted text' button. Attachments inline at top of body with small thumbnails.

design_tokens.colors: Collapsed summary bg: oklch(0.97 0.005 250). Quoted text bg: oklch(0.95 0.005 250) (slight indent).
design_tokens.motion: Expand: 200ms ease-out height. Quoted reveal: instant.

component_architecture: <EmailThread messages>. <Message expanded /> renders collapsed or full.
interaction_details: Latest message expanded by default. Earlier messages collapsed.
Click collapsed message: expands with body + attachments.
Quoted text auto-collapsed; click '••• Show quoted text' to expand.
Attachments inline at top of body with thumbnails.
accessibility_considerations: Each message is an article. Collapsed rows are buttons. Quoted-text toggle has aria-expanded.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Latest expanded by default — that's the message users want to read. 2) Quoted text collapsed — kills the wall of repeated content. 3) Attachments inline at top — not buried at the bottom.

title: Slash command menu inside chat-like input
design_brief: Building slash commands for an AI chat or messaging input (think Discord /commands, Slack /commands, Linear's slash). Pain point: most slash menus don't filter as you type or require mouse.
ux_reasoning: Type / in the input: dropdown appears anchored to caret. Continue typing to filter ('/im' shows /image, /import). Arrow keys navigate; Enter or Tab inserts (with placeholder for args). Esc dismisses BUT keeps the typed '/' so user can finish typing literal slash.

design_tokens.colors: Dropdown bg: oklch(0.18 0.005 250) text white. Selected: oklch(0.28 0.005 250).

interaction_details: Type /: dropdown opens with all commands.
Continue typing: filters by prefix.
Tab inserts command name + space; Enter executes.
Esc preserves '/' so user can type literal slash.
accessibility_considerations: Expose Slash command menu inside chat-like input through visible labels, keyboard or non-gesture controls, screen-reader status text, and reduced-motion behavior where motion is used.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Tab inserts (Enter executes) — separates exploration from action. 2) Esc preserves typed slash — never punishes accidental trigger. 3) Anchored to caret, not to input bottom.

title: Activity feed with read/unread markers and inline mention previews
design_brief: Building activity feed (think GitHub notifications, Linear inbox). Pain point: most feeds are walls of text without clear unread/read distinction.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Mark all as read prominent (encourages skipping)
Generic 'User X did Y in Z' format
ux_reasoning: Unread items have bold sender + 4px left border in user color. Items group by source (thread, project). Mentions show inline preview of context. Click marks as read but keeps in list.

design_tokens.colors: Unread left border: oklch(0.55 0.18 250). Unread text: oklch(0.2 0.01 250) medium. Read text: oklch(0.55 0.005 250).

interaction_details: Unread items have colored left border in sender's user-color + bold sender name.
Inline preview if it's a mention or comment.
Click: marks read (border fades over 200ms) + opens target.
Items stay in list after read.
accessibility_considerations: For Activity feed with read/unread markers and inline mention previews, provide semantic labels, keyboard or non-gesture controls, visible focus, status/error announcements, and a reduced-motion path when animation is involved.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Sender-color border for unread — at-a-glance who acted. 2) Inline preview of mentions — context without click. 3) Read items stay in list — history preserved.

title: Star rating with hover preview and intent confirmation
design_brief: Building star rating (think IMDb, Yelp, App Store). Pain point: most ratings commit instantly, no preview.
ux_reasoning: Hovering stars previews the rating; clicking commits. Half-star precision via mouseX position within a star. Already-rated state shows existing rating; click again to change. Small 'undo' link appears for 5s after rating.

design_tokens.colors: Star filled: oklch(0.8 0.18 80) (warm gold). Empty: oklch(0.88 0.005 250).
design_tokens.motion: Hover fill: instant. Click pulse: 200ms scale 1.1 → 1.
design_tokens.typography: Type scale for Star rating with hover preview and intent confirmation: readable body text, compact labels, and no viewport-width font scaling.

interaction_details: Hover stars: preview rating in real time.
Mouse position within star: half-star precision.
Click: commits.
After commit: small 'undo' link for 5s (could be added).
accessibility_considerations: For Star rating with hover preview and intent confirmation, provide semantic labels, keyboard or non-gesture controls, visible focus, status/error announcements, and a reduced-motion path when animation is involved.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Half-star via mouseX position — precise without decimals UI. 2) Hover previews; click commits — separates intent. 3) Single clipPath split for fill — visually exact half-star.

title: Bullet chart for KPIs (target/actual/threshold) replacing gauges
design_brief: Building KPI display (think Stephen Few's bullet chart, Tableau dashboards). Pain point: most KPI displays use circular gauges that waste space and obscure scale.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Generic progress bar with no target line
ux_reasoning: Horizontal bullet chart per KPI: bar showing actual, perpendicular line at target, qualitative ranges as subtle bg bands (poor/fair/good). Compact, scannable.

design_tokens.colors: Actual: oklch(0.3 0.005 250). Target line: oklch(0.55 0.18 250). Range good: oklch(0.92 0.04 145). Fair: oklch(0.94 0.04 60). Poor: oklch(0.95 0.04 25).
design_tokens.motion: Bar fill: 400ms ease-out on mount only.

interaction_details: Compact horizontal bar: actual fills foreground; target as perpendicular line.
Background bands show poor/fair/good ranges.
Numeric value visible above bar.
accessibility_considerations: Target line has aria-label with value. Underlying values available in SR table.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Bullet chart (Stephen Few) — replaces dial gauges with denser, more scannable form. 2) Target line + range bands in one bar. 3) Same scale per row enables comparison.

title: Persistent 'first time' hint that nests inside the relevant control
design_brief: Building first-time hints for specific controls (think Things 3's quiet tips, Apple Notes). Pain point: tooltips are noisy + dismissive; first-time guidance often gets shown once and lost.
ux_reasoning: Below a complex control, a small italic ghost text reveals on first hover/focus ('hint: drag to reorder'). Dismisses after 3 successful uses of the control OR after explicit dismiss. Persists across sessions via localStorage.

design_tokens.colors: Ghost text: oklch(0.55 0.005 250).

interaction_details: Hover/focus the control: hint appears below.
Each interaction increments success count.
After N successes: hint never shows again.
Persists per-user via localStorage.
accessibility_considerations: Hint text is real text, not a tooltip — readable by SR users when present.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Success-counter dismissal — never sticks around after user clearly knows. 2) Persists per-user across sessions. 3) Inline ghost text — no popover chrome.

title: Multi-file drag-and-drop with per-file progress and reorder
design_brief: Building file dropzone (think Google Drive upload, Notion file upload). Pain point: drop UX is often binary and doesn't let users prune files before upload.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Single drop event with no preview
Files can't be removed before upload
ux_reasoning: Drop reveals a queue of files BELOW the dropzone — each with thumbnail (if image), name, size, remove button. Reorder via drag handle. Upload button uploads all. Per-file progress during upload.

design_tokens.colors: Dropzone bg active: oklch(0.94 0.04 250). Queue item bg: oklch(0.97 0.005 250).
design_tokens.motion: Dropzone active: 150ms bg transition. Queue item enter: 200ms slide.

interaction_details: Drag over: dropzone highlights.
Drop: files queue below with thumb + name + remove.
Drag handle reorders.
Upload button explicit.
accessibility_considerations: File input has label. Remove buttons aria-labelled. Reduced-motion: file-in animation disabled.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Queue separate from drop event — prune before upload. 2) Per-file remove — no all-or-nothing. 3) Reorder via drag handle — user controls upload order.

title: Globe with rotating arc visualizations of flights/connections
design_brief: Building a globe visualization (think GitHub's contribution globe, Stripe's atlas, airline route maps). Pain point: many globes are static or over-animated.
ux_reasoning: Globe slowly auto-rotates (1 rev / 60s). Connection arcs animate ONCE on appear (draw-in over 1s). Hover arc highlights both endpoints with city labels. Click arc: details panel.

design_tokens.colors: Globe sphere: oklch(0.20 0.04 230). Land: oklch(0.35 0.04 230). Arc: oklch(0.7 0.18 200).
design_tokens.motion: Rotate: 0.001 rad/frame. Arc draw-in: 1s once per arc.
design_tokens.typography: Type scale for Globe with rotating arc visualizations of flights/connections: readable body text, compact labels, and no viewport-width font scaling.

interaction_details: Slow auto-rotate (60s per rev).
Drag to manually orbit; release returns to auto-rotate.
Arcs draw in once on appear.
Hover arc: endpoints labeled.
accessibility_considerations: For Globe with rotating arc visualizations of flights/connections, provide semantic labels, keyboard or non-gesture controls, visible focus, status/error announcements, and a reduced-motion path when animation is involved.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Slow auto-rotate (not fast) — alive without dizzying. 2) Arc draw-in once per arc — story moment, not loop. 3) Hover surfaces context — globe is interrogable.

title: Comments thread with reaction picker on long-press (mobile) and hover (desktop)
design_brief: Building comments with reactions (think Slack, Discord, iMessage). Pain point: reaction picker UX differs between mobile and desktop poorly.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Hover-only on desktop (touch users can't react)
ux_reasoning: Mobile: long-press 200ms on a message reveals reaction picker as a horizontal rail above the message. Tap an emoji to react. Desktop: hover-reveal of small reaction button on message; click opens picker.

design_tokens.colors: Picker bg: white with shadow. Selected reaction: 2px ring brand.
design_tokens.typography: Type scale for Comments thread with reaction picker on long-press (mobile) and hover (desktop): readable body text, compact labels, and no viewport-width font scaling.

interaction_details: Mobile: long-press message (200ms) opens reaction picker above.
Desktop: hover message reveals small reaction button; click opens picker.
Tap emoji: adds reaction; picker closes.
Existing reactions shown as chips below message with count.
accessibility_considerations: For Comments thread with reaction picker on long-press (mobile) and hover (desktop), provide semantic labels, keyboard or non-gesture controls, visible focus, status/error announcements, and a reduced-motion path when animation is involved.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Modality-aware reveal — long-press touch / hover mouse. 2) Picker as horizontal rail (not modal). 3) Existing reactions click to toggle.

title: Skip-to-content link that's visible only on focus
design_brief: Building skip-links for keyboard nav (think a11y best practice, gov.uk). Pain point: most skip-links are invisible to sighted users AND often forgotten.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Permanently hidden (some browsers don't focus invisible)
Generic 'Skip to content' that doesn't say what's skipped
ux_reasoning: Skip link is the FIRST focusable element. Visually hidden until focused (then positioned visibly at top-left with bg + ring). Tab from URL bar reveals it. Multiple skip links: 'Skip to navigation', 'Skip to main content', 'Skip to footer'.

design_tokens.colors: On focus: oklch(0.18 0.005 250) bg, white text.
design_tokens.motion: Meaningful 120-180ms transitions for Skip-to-content link that's visible only on focus, disabled under prefers-reduced-motion.

component_architecture: <SkipLinks targets>. Renders an ordered list of skip links.
interaction_details: First Tab from URL bar reveals first skip link.
Click or Enter: jumps to target with focus management.
Visible only when focused.
Multiple links for different regions.
accessibility_considerations: Real anchor links. sr-only + focus:not-sr-only pattern is canonical Tailwind. Works in all browsers including Safari.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Visible only on focus — clutter-free yet keyboard-discoverable. 2) Multiple skip targets — sophisticated docs/dashboards need more than one. 3) Specific labels ('Skip to navigation', not just 'Skip').

title: View transitions API for SPA route changes
design_brief: Using the View Transitions API (Chrome 111+) for smooth SPA navigation (think how native apps animate between screens). Pain point: SPA routes feel jarring without help.
ux_reasoning: Use document.startViewTransition() to animate between routes. Mark shared elements (e.g., article thumbnail → article hero) with view-transition-name so they morph smoothly across pages. Default cross-fade for other content.

design_tokens.colors: Semantic surface/text/accent/error tokens for View transitions API for SPA route changes, checked against WCAG AA.
design_tokens.spacing: 4px spacing scale with stable component dimensions for View transitions API for SPA route changes.
design_tokens.motion: View transitions: 250ms ease-out default. Shared elements morph in same window.
design_tokens.typography: Type scale for View transitions API for SPA route changes: readable body text, compact labels, and no viewport-width font scaling.

component_architecture: useViewTransition(callback) hook wraps state changes that should animate.
interaction_details: Click article in list: transitions to detail; thumbnail morphs into hero image.
Default fade for other content.
Reduced-motion: instant.
Browser handles all animation via native API.
accessibility_considerations: View Transitions API respects prefers-reduced-motion. Falls back to instant if API unavailable.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Browser-native API — best perf, declarative shared-element transitions. 2) Falls back gracefully for older browsers. 3) Single CSS property (view-transition-name) wires the morph.

title: Hand-drawn aesthetic prototype tool with wobbly lines
design_brief: Building a sketch/wireframe tool aesthetic (think Balsamiq, Excalidraw, tldraw's sketchy mode). Pain point: 'sketchy' is often a thin gimmick, not consistent.
ux_reasoning: Everything sketchy: borders are slightly wobbly SVG paths (not straight), filled colors are crayon-textured (svg displacement filter), fonts are hand-written sans (Architects Daughter, Caveat). Aesthetic is consistent across buttons, shapes, text.

design_tokens.colors: Ink: oklch(0.18 0.005 30). Paper: oklch(0.97 0.02 80) (warm cream). Accent colors: muted oklch hues.

component_architecture: <SketchButton><SketchPath /></SketchButton>. SVG paths with random control point jitter.
interaction_details: Borders are wobbly SVG paths (random jitter).
Cream paper bg, dark ink, hand-drawn font.
Hover: slight rotation (1deg).
Aesthetic consistent across all elements.
accessibility_considerations: For Hand-drawn aesthetic prototype tool with wobbly lines, provide semantic labels, keyboard or non-gesture controls, visible focus, status/error announcements, and a reduced-motion path when animation is involved.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Real wobbly SVG paths (jittered control points) — not just rounded rectangles. 2) Hand-written font + cream paper bg — consistent aesthetic. 3) Hover rotation feels like a sticky note.

title: Notification center with type-grouped sections and bulk actions
design_brief: Building a notification center (think iOS Notification Center, Slack notification panel, Linear inbox). Pain point: flat list of mixed-type notifications is hard to triage.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Flat chronological list of all notification types
Notifications mixed across apps without grouping
ux_reasoning: Notifications group by source (project, person). Each group has a count badge + collapse chevron. Bulk actions per group (mark all read, mute, archive). Inline reply for messages.

design_tokens.colors: Group bg: subtle bg per category color. Unread dot: brand.

component_architecture: <NotificationCenter notifications onAction>. Groups computed by source.
interaction_details: Notifications grouped by source.
Each group has count badge + collapse + 'Mark all read'.
Unread dot per item.
Inline reply for messages (could be added).
accessibility_considerations: For Notification center with type-grouped sections and bulk actions, provide semantic labels, keyboard or non-gesture controls, visible focus, status/error announcements, and a reduced-motion path when animation is involved.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Source-grouped, not chronological — triage by context. 2) Bulk actions per group, not global. 3) Unread count per group + per item.

title: Settings command with Cmd+, and Cmd+L for navigation
design_brief: Building app-wide keyboard shortcuts inspired by macOS conventions (Cmd+, opens settings, Cmd+L focuses URL/search). Pain point: most web apps don't honor OS conventions.
ux_reasoning: App-wide shortcuts: Cmd+, opens settings, Cmd+K opens command palette, Cmd+L focuses search/address-like input, Cmd+/ shows shortcut help. Borrow from macOS. Avoid browser conflicts.

design_tokens.colors: Semantic surface/text/accent/error tokens for Settings command with Cmd+, and Cmd+L for navigation, checked against WCAG AA.
design_tokens.spacing: 4px spacing scale with stable component dimensions for Settings command with Cmd+, and Cmd+L for navigation.
design_tokens.motion: Meaningful 120-180ms transitions for Settings command with Cmd+, and Cmd+L for navigation, disabled under prefers-reduced-motion.
design_tokens.typography: Type scale for Settings command with Cmd+, and Cmd+L for navigation: readable body text, compact labels, and no viewport-width font scaling.

interaction_details: Cmd+,: opens Settings.
Cmd+K: opens command palette.
Cmd+L: focuses primary search.
Cmd+/: opens shortcut help.
accessibility_considerations: Shortcuts complement (don't replace) menu navigation. Help overlay (Cmd+/) lists all.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Honors macOS conventions (Cmd+, for settings is universal). 2) Help overlay (Cmd+/) is discovery. 3) Hook is composable — pages add/remove handlers.

title: Multi-user typing indicators with batched 'three are typing'
design_brief: Building typing indicators (think Slack, iMessage). Pain point: multiple typers shown verbosely ('Alice, Bob, and Carol are typing...').
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Three-dots animation that's a single GIF
ux_reasoning: 1 typer: 'Alice is typing'. 2: 'Alice and Bob are typing'. 3+: 'Several people are typing'. Update on broadcasts; auto-dismiss 3s after last keystroke from each. Three-dot animation is real, looping every 1.2s.

design_tokens.colors: Text: oklch(0.55 0.005 250). Dots: same.

interaction_details: 1 typer: name shown.
2: 'A and B are typing'.
3+: 'Several people are typing'.
Stops after 3s since last keystroke.
Three dots animate with stagger.
accessibility_considerations: Expose Multi-user typing indicators with batched 'three are typing' through visible labels, keyboard or non-gesture controls, screen-reader status text, and reduced-motion behavior where motion is used.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Counts 3+ as 'several people' — doesn't spam names. 2) Auto-dismiss tied to per-user keystroke time. 3) Real dot bounce animation, not GIF.

title: Swipe-action row with double-distance for destructive
design_brief: Building swipe actions on a list row (think Mail.app, Things 3). Pain point: destructive actions are too easy to swipe.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Same swipe distance for archive and delete
ux_reasoning: Short swipe (60px): reveals safe action (archive). Long swipe (160px+): reveals destructive (delete) with bg color shift to red + haptic. Release at safe-threshold: archives. Release at destructive: requires confirmation tap.

design_tokens.colors: Safe: oklch(0.75 0.16 145). Destructive: oklch(0.6 0.18 25).
design_tokens.typography: Type scale for Swipe-action row with double-distance for destructive: readable body text, compact labels, and no viewport-width font scaling.

interaction_details: Swipe left: reveals archive bg (green).
Continue past 160px: bg flips to red 'Delete' + haptic.
Release at archive zone: archives.
Release at delete zone: confirm prompt.
accessibility_considerations: Keyboard equivalent: focus + Cmd+A archive, Cmd+Delete delete. Reduced-motion: snap-back instant.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Two-zone swipe — safe vs destructive distinguished by distance. 2) Haptic at destructive threshold — body knows. 3) Destructive requires confirm — never one-swipe delete.

title: Pareto chart (bar + cumulative line) for finding 80/20
design_brief: Building Pareto chart for analyzing top contributors (think error count by source, sales by product). Pain point: bar charts alone don't show cumulative %.
ux_reasoning: Bars sorted descending. Right axis is cumulative percentage. Line overlaid showing cumulative. 80% horizontal reference line. Hovering a bar shows individual + cumulative contribution.

design_tokens.colors: Bars: oklch(0.55 0.18 250). Cumulative line: oklch(0.55 0.18 25). 80% reference: oklch(0.55 0.005 250) dashed.
design_tokens.motion: Bars animate up on mount (300ms stagger). Line draws in after.

component_architecture: <ParetoChart data />. Sorts by value descending, computes cumulative.
interaction_details: Bars sorted descending.
Cumulative line overlaid.
80% reference line (the 'Pareto point').
Hover any bar: individual + cumulative %.
accessibility_considerations: Expose Pareto chart (bar + cumulative line) for finding 80/20 through visible labels, keyboard or non-gesture controls, screen-reader status text, and reduced-motion behavior where motion is used.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Single chart shows individual + cumulative — the 80/20 pattern is visually obvious. 2) Sorted bars enforce ranking. 3) 80% reference makes the Pareto cut explicit.

title: Email verify with magic link that opens current tab
design_brief: Building email verification (think Notion, Linear sign-in via email link). Pain point: magic links open new tabs leaving original signup tab in limbo.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Magic link opens new tab; signup tab forgotten
No way to resend without going back
ux_reasoning: After signup, show 'check your email' page with a code-entry input AND a passive listener that detects when the link is clicked (via BroadcastChannel from the link's tab to this tab). Original tab auto-completes once verified.

design_tokens.colors: Verified state: oklch(0.55 0.14 145) bg fade.

component_architecture: <EmailVerifyPage email onVerified>. BroadcastChannel listener for cross-tab.
interaction_details: Show 'check your email' page with code input.
BroadcastChannel listens for verify event from link's tab.
On verify: page auto-completes with success state and redirects.
Manual code entry as fallback (autocomplete='one-time-code' triggers iOS SMS autofill).
accessibility_considerations: Code input has aria-label + autoComplete='one-time-code'. Verified state announced via aria-live.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) BroadcastChannel cross-tab sync — original tab auto-completes when link clicked anywhere. 2) Code entry as parallel path — supports both flows. 3) autoComplete='one-time-code' enables iOS auto-fill.

title: Smart number input with locale-aware formatting
design_brief: Building number input that displays formatted but stores raw (think currency in checkout, quantity in cart). Pain point: typing 1234 should display as 1,234 but parse back to number on submit.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Inline format that breaks cursor position
No locale awareness (US uses , and ., others use space/comma)
Validation that fires on each char
ux_reasoning: Type digits; display formats per locale. Cursor position preserved via measure offset. Locale comes from Intl.NumberFormat. Negative supported via leading minus.

design_tokens.colors: Standard input.
design_tokens.motion: Meaningful 120-180ms transitions for Smart number input with locale-aware formatting, disabled under prefers-reduced-motion.

interaction_details: Type digits: displays formatted per locale (1,234 in US).
Stores raw number internally.
Negative supported via leading minus.
Cursor stays at end of input (simple impl).
accessibility_considerations: Expose Smart number input with locale-aware formatting through visible labels, keyboard or non-gesture controls, screen-reader status text, and reduced-motion behavior where motion is used.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Locale-aware formatting via Intl.NumberFormat. 2) Display is formatted; underlying value is number. 3) Strict digit filtering prevents bad input.

title: 3D pricing comparison with extruded tiers and hover-elevate
design_brief: Building 3D pricing page (think creative agency sites, Vercel Enterprise comparison page with depth). Pain point: pricing pages are often flat tables that don't communicate hierarchy.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Cards that don't differ in scale
ux_reasoning: 3 pricing tiers as extruded cards on a low-poly stage. Recommended tier is taller. Hover elevates a card and slightly rotates camera toward it. Click commits a tier.

design_tokens.colors: Card materials: matte oklch. Recommended: subtle metallic gold accent.
design_tokens.spacing: Cards arranged in a row at slight angle.
design_tokens.typography: Type scale for 3D pricing comparison with extruded tiers and hover-elevate: readable body text, compact labels, and no viewport-width font scaling.

interaction_details: 3 tiers as extruded cards on a low-poly stage.
Recommended tier is taller + slightly metallic.
Hover elevates card 10%.
Click commits tier.
accessibility_considerations: sr-only pricing table available. Each tier card has keyboard focus + Enter.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Recommended tier physically taller — no banner needed. 2) Hover elevates (not scales) — feels physical. 3) Materials communicate value (matte vs metallic).

title: Chat input that grows up to N lines then scrolls
design_brief: Building message input (think Slack, Discord, WhatsApp Web). Pain point: textarea that's fixed height OR grows infinitely.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Grows infinitely (pushes content out of view)
ux_reasoning: Auto-height grows with content up to 6 lines (~144px), then scrolls internally. Shrinks back when content removed. Shift+Enter = newline; Enter = send.

design_tokens.colors: Border focus: brand.

interaction_details: Auto-grows up to 6 lines, then scrolls internally.
Shrinks back when content removed.
Enter sends; Shift+Enter newline.
accessibility_considerations: Expose Chat input that grows up to N lines then scrolls through visible labels, keyboard or non-gesture controls, screen-reader status text, and reduced-motion behavior where motion is used.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Auto-height with maxLines cap — never blocks UI. 2) Shrinks on delete — reciprocal. 3) 16px font on mobile (iOS zoom prevention).

title: Color contrast checker on a custom design palette
design_brief: Building a color palette UI for designers to ensure WCAG contrast (think Stripe colors, Adobe Color, Tailwind picker). Pain point: designers pick colors that don't pass contrast.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Generic 'WCAG AAA' badge without numeric ratio
ux_reasoning: For each fg/bg pair, show ratio (e.g., 4.7:1) + WCAG levels (AA / AAA for normal text and for large text). Failing pairs get a red badge with the closest passing alternative suggested.

design_tokens.colors: Pass green oklch(0.55 0.14 145). Fail red oklch(0.6 0.18 25).
design_tokens.motion: Meaningful 120-180ms transitions for Color contrast checker on a custom design palette, disabled under prefers-reduced-motion.

interaction_details: Each pair: preview, ratio, WCAG level badges.
Failing pairs visually marked.
Could extend to suggest adjustments.
accessibility_considerations: This IS the a11y tool. Output is data; presentation respects own contrast rules.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Real WCAG ratio calculation (luminance formula). 2) Both normal-text and large-text levels shown. 3) Visual + numeric — fast scan.

title: Sequential image carousel with eager-load adjacent images
design_brief: Building an image carousel with smooth navigation (think product galleries, instagram). Pain point: clicking next shows blank then loads.
ux_reasoning: Load current + 2 adjacent images. As user navigates, slide window forward (load next-next, drop prev-prev). Smooth crossfade between images.

design_tokens.colors: Background placeholder: dominant color.
design_tokens.typography: Type scale for Sequential image carousel with eager-load adjacent images: readable body text, compact labels, and no viewport-width font scaling.

interaction_details: Eager-loads current + 2 adjacent (prev, next).
Click next: image already in DOM, crossfades in.
Dominant color fills bg during any load.
No spinner ever.
accessibility_considerations: Expose Sequential image carousel with eager-load adjacent images through visible labels, keyboard or non-gesture controls, screen-reader status text, and reduced-motion behavior where motion is used.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) 3-image render window — next click is instant. 2) Dominant-color fallback. 3) Crossfade, not slide — calm.

title: Risograph-style poster generator with overprint colors
design_brief: Building a riso-aesthetic UI for an indie publishing platform or zine maker (think Hato Press, Are.na's events). Pain point: most 'riso' interfaces are pastel washes, missing the overprint texture.
ux_reasoning: Riso real: limited spot colors that overprint to create third colors where they overlap. SVG with mix-blend-mode: multiply between layered colored shapes creates the effect. Subtle paper noise via SVG turbulence filter. Display font with rough edges.

design_tokens.colors: Riso palette: fluorescent pink oklch(0.75 0.22 0), risograph blue oklch(0.55 0.18 240), yellow oklch(0.88 0.18 95). Paper: oklch(0.96 0.02 90) warm.
design_tokens.typography: Display: condensed bold (Druk, Compacta). Body: serif 14px.

interaction_details: Two overlapping colored circles use mix-blend-mode: multiply — overlap creates third color naturally.
Paper noise via SVG turbulence at low opacity.
Condensed bold display font.
Warm cream paper bg.
accessibility_considerations: For Risograph-style poster generator with overprint colors, provide semantic labels, keyboard or non-gesture controls, visible focus, status/error announcements, and a reduced-motion path when animation is involved.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) mix-blend-mode: multiply for real overprint behavior. 2) SVG turbulence noise for paper grain. 3) Warm cream bg (oklch 0.96 0.02 90) — never pure white.

title: GitHub-style commit history with author + sha + tag filtering
design_brief: Building git history view (think GitHub commits, GitLab, Sourcetree). Pain point: long lists, hard to filter by author or branch.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: No way to filter by author
ux_reasoning: Commits grouped by day. Each commit row: avatar (24px), author name, message (truncated), tags/branches as chips, sha (mono 7 chars), relative time. Filter by author / branch via dropdown.

design_tokens.colors: Tag chips: pastel hues. Sha: oklch(0.55 0.005 250).
design_tokens.motion: Meaningful 120-180ms transitions for GitHub-style commit history with author + sha + tag filtering, disabled under prefers-reduced-motion.

interaction_details: Commits grouped by day with sticky header.
Each commit: avatar + message + author + sha + tags.
Click any to view diff.
accessibility_considerations: For GitHub-style commit history with author + sha + tag filtering, provide semantic labels, keyboard or non-gesture controls, visible focus, status/error announcements, and a reduced-motion path when animation is involved.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Sticky day headers — scannable timeline. 2) Avatar + author + sha all on one row — git's identity fingerprint. 3) Branch tags inline at right.

title: Multi-select with Shift-range and Cmd-individual
design_brief: Building multi-select (think macOS Finder, Linear issues, Spotify playlists). Pain point: most web multi-select ignores OS conventions.
ux_reasoning: Click selects single. Shift+click selects range from last anchor. Cmd/Ctrl+click toggles individual. Cmd+A selects all. Esc clears.

design_tokens.colors: Selected bg: oklch(0.94 0.04 250).
design_tokens.motion: Meaningful 120-180ms transitions for Multi-select with Shift-range and Cmd-individual, disabled under prefers-reduced-motion.
design_tokens.typography: Type scale for Multi-select with Shift-range and Cmd-individual: readable body text, compact labels, and no viewport-width font scaling.

interaction_details: Click: select single (replace).
Shift+click: extend range from anchor.
Cmd/Ctrl+click: toggle individual in/out of selection.
Cmd+A: select all. Esc: clear.
accessibility_considerations: For Multi-select with Shift-range and Cmd-individual, provide semantic labels, keyboard or non-gesture controls, visible focus, status/error announcements, and a reduced-motion path when animation is involved.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Honors macOS multi-select conventions. 2) Anchor preserved for range extends. 3) Generic hook works with any item type.

title: Live edit indicators in a kanban card (who's typing the description)
design_brief: Building card description with live edit awareness (think Asana, Trello, Linear card detail). Pain point: two people editing description = lost text.
ux_reasoning: When user A focuses description field, others see field with A's color border + 'A is writing'. Others CAN still type (last writer wins, with history preservation). All edits persist as numbered revisions accessible via 'History' link.

design_tokens.colors: Edit border per user color.
design_tokens.typography: Type scale for Live edit indicators in a kanban card (who's typing the description): readable body text, compact labels, and no viewport-width font scaling.

interaction_details: Other user editing: field border in their color + 'X is writing' label.
Both can type — last writer wins.
History link reveals all revisions.
accessibility_considerations: Expose Live edit indicators in a kanban card (who's typing the description) through visible labels, keyboard or non-gesture controls, screen-reader status text, and reduced-motion behavior where motion is used.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Soft lock with visible attribution. 2) History link makes overwrites recoverable. 3) Border color = attribution.

title: Save state indicator: 'Saved' → 'Editing...' → 'Saving...' → 'Saved'
design_brief: Building auto-save indicator for a doc editor (think Google Docs, Notion). Pain point: vague 'unsaved changes' badges, no clarity on when save fires.
ux_reasoning: Cycle through states: 'Saved · 2m ago' (idle) → 'Editing...' (user typed) → 'Saving...' (debounced fire) → 'Saved · just now'. Single small text in the header with subtle color shift.

design_tokens.colors: Saved: oklch(0.55 0.005 250). Editing: oklch(0.55 0.005 250). Saving: oklch(0.55 0.18 250). Error: oklch(0.55 0.18 25).

interaction_details: Type: state goes to 'Editing...' immediately.
After 1.5s debounce: 'Saving...' fires save.
On success: 'Saved · just now' (refreshes time).
On error: 'Failed to save' in red.
accessibility_considerations: For Save state indicator: 'Saved' → 'Editing...' → 'Saving...' → 'Saved', provide semantic labels, keyboard or non-gesture controls, visible focus, status/error announcements, and a reduced-motion path when animation is involved.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Cycle communicates the entire save lifecycle in one indicator. 2) Relative time updates without polling. 3) Single hook + display — composable.

title: Activity histogram with brushable time selector
design_brief: Building histogram for time-distributed events (think GitHub commits by hour-of-day, Apple Health steps). Pain point: histograms are static.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Bars without exact value on hover
ux_reasoning: Bars per time bin. Brush a range to filter events shown elsewhere on page. Hover bar: tooltip with exact count + time range.

design_tokens.colors: Bars: oklch(0.55 0.18 250). Selected bars (in brush range): brighter. Outside brush: dimmed.
design_tokens.typography: Type scale for Activity histogram with brushable time selector: readable body text, compact labels, and no viewport-width font scaling.

interaction_details: Drag horizontally: brush a range; bars in range stay bright, others dim.
Brush sends range to parent (filters related data).
Click outside brush: clears it (could be added).
Hover bar for exact count.
accessibility_considerations: Expose Activity histogram with brushable time selector through visible labels, keyboard or non-gesture controls, screen-reader status text, and reduced-motion behavior where motion is used.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Drag-brush as primary interaction — same gesture as Excel/Tableau. 2) Other bars dim — focus on selection. 3) Range broadcasts to other charts.

title: Recent activity timeline as 'you can do these things' demo
design_brief: Building a 'your activity' timeline used as soft onboarding (think GitHub's activity, Apple Fitness summary). Pain point: empty 'no activity' is a dead end.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Generic 'Create activity to see it here'
Boring chronological list with no narrative
ux_reasoning: If no activity: show a sample activity that demonstrates 3 things the user could do ('Create a project', 'Invite teammate', 'Set up a goal'). Each is a tappable mini-action that creates real activity.

design_tokens.colors: Sample bg: oklch(0.97 0.005 250). Real: white.
design_tokens.motion: Meaningful 120-180ms transitions for Recent activity timeline as 'you can do these things' demo, disabled under prefers-reduced-motion.

interaction_details: Empty activity shows sample suggestions as tappable rows.
Real activity replaces sample as user does things.
Suggestions are real actions (not just placeholders).
accessibility_considerations: Expose Recent activity timeline as 'you can do these things' demo through visible labels, keyboard or non-gesture controls, screen-reader status text, and reduced-motion behavior where motion is used.
what_makes_this_distinctive: 1) Empty state IS the suggestion list — actionable, not illustrative. 2) Suggestions demo real features. 3) Smooth transition to real activity (no separate empty/populated views).
